summary.conclusion: HyperFrames is more reliable for the default local MP4 render path on this devbox. Editframe has a capable timeline/editor/API model and can render locally via browser VP9 on this box, but its published CLI default path hard-codes/chooses AVC and fails because this Chrome WebCodecs build does not support the AVC encoder config.
summary.caveat: This is not a blind final-video-quality benchmark. It is a devbox execution and workflow eval over one precise-timing fixture plus source/docs evidence.

stream.codec_long_name: H.264 / AVC / MPEG-4 AVC / MPEG-4 part 10

categories.name: Visual output quality
categories.note: Not blindly judged. Both controlled fixtures produced screenshots; this run should not decide taste/video quality.

categories.name: Timing and motion control
categories.note: Both can seek exact timestamps. HyperFrames validated and snapshotted the GSAP timeline through first-party CLI; Editframe snapshotted via ef-timegroup.seek and rendered through browser API.

categories.name: Timeline and editability
categories.note: Editframe has richer public editor primitives; HyperFrames source remains plain HTML/data attributes and passed lint/validate.

categories.name: Agent success rate
categories.note: HyperFrames fresh init plus lint/validate/snapshot/render passed. Editframe fresh init passed, but advertised --skip-skills flag failed and default CLI render failed on AVC.

categories.name: Media handling
categories.note: This devbox run did not exercise external media, audio, captions, or alpha.

categories.name: Render fidelity
categories.note: HyperFrames produced default H.264 MP4 at 1920x1080/30fps/10s. Editframe CLI default failed; browser VP9 fallback produced valid 1920x1080/30fps/10s MP4.

categories.name: Performance and cost
categories.note: Editframe VP9 browser render elapsed 1280ms; HyperFrames draft H.264 render command elapsed 5971ms. Codecs/render paths differ, so this is directional only.

categories.name: Observability and debugging
categories.note: HyperFrames surfaced lint/contrast checks and progress. Editframe error was clear, but the CLI left a 28-byte output file and exposes no codec flag.

categories.name: Deployment and API ergonomics
categories.note: Editframe public docs include cloud/API render jobs, file processing, webhooks, and CPU/GPU backend selection. This run did not authenticate to test it live.

categories.name: Human iteration workflow
categories.note: Not exercised with human editors. Public docs suggest both have timeline/editor workflows; Editframe has broader standalone editor widgets.
